hypothesis: Shifted-center convolutions everywhere in the incumbent ConvNeXt architecture may suppress boundary artifacts by never sampling beyond the domain.
Applying shifted-center convolutions only at the finest-resolution blocks may target the boundary artifact source without changing the whole operator.
Applying shifted-center convolutions at the two finest-resolution scales may better contain boundary contamination that spreads inward across multiple high-resolution stages.
Shifted-center convolutions everywhere with a milder dilation cycle may preserve the no-exterior boundary treatment while reducing aggressive one-sided receptive field growth.
A standard two-conv U-Net block with shifted-center convolutions everywhere may handle the one-sided boundary operator more cleanly than ConvNeXt depthwise blocks.
